name: Kalsee
description_short: KALSEE Credit Union is a federally insured, member-owned financial institution headquartered in Kalamazoo, MI, serving 20,235 members across 5 branches with $147.6M in assets.
description_long:
KALSEE Credit Union was chartered on January 1, 1954, and is headquartered in Kalamazoo, Michigan. As a federally insured state-chartered credit union with $147.6 million in total assets, it operates as a nonprofit financial cooperative serving over 20,235 members through 5 branch locations across southwest Michigan. The institution is regulated by the NCUA (National Credit Union Administration) and holds charter number 61219.

KALSEE offers a comprehensive range of consumer and business financial services including checking and savings accounts, mortgages, personal and business loans, health savings accounts, certificates of deposit, bill payment services, ATM access, debit cards, and money orders. Members can access electronic banking through online home banking, mobile banking, and automated phone systems. The credit union also provides financial counseling and insurance/investment sales services, along with overdraft protection and check verification tools.

KALSEE's membership is geographically restricted to individuals who live, work, or are educated in eight specific Michigan counties (Allegan, Barry, Branch, Calhoun, Cass, Kalamazoo, St. Joseph, and Van Buren), or who receive retirement income and live in Kalamazoo County or adjacent counties. This regional focus distinguishes it as a community-based institution with deep local roots rather than a national provider. The low minimum deposit requirement of $25 to open an account makes membership accessible to consumers with limited savings.

While KALSEE provides legitimate credit union services with NCUA deposit insurance protection, potential members should note that membership eligibility is limited by geography, and online reviews mention accessibility issues with phone support. The credit union does not participate in the Federal Home Loan Bank system and carries no low-income designation despite its cooperative structure. Service availability and responsiveness appear inconsistent based on member feedback. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Business loans and business share accounts
Online bill payment and account transfers
pros: NCUA deposit insurance protects all member deposits
Low $25 minimum deposit requirement to open an account
5 convenient branch locations across Kalamazoo and Portage area
Multiple electronic banking channels: online, mobile, ATM, and phone-based systems
No-surcharge ATM network access for members
Federally insured state-chartered credit union with 70+ years of operational history
Offers business loans and services for small business members
cons: Membership restricted to 8 specific Michigan counties or retirement income recipients — geographic eligibility barrier for many consumers
Member complaint about chronic phone line busy signals suggesting poor customer service accessibility
Not a member of Federal Home Loan Bank, potentially limiting mortgage lending flexibility
No low-income credit union designation despite nonprofit structure
Minimal online ratings and reviews (listed as 'Not Yet Rated' on BranchSpot)
best_for: Residents or employees in southwest Michigan counties seeking a locally-rooted financial institution
Small business owners in eligible service areas needing business loans and accounts
Retirees receiving pension or Social Security income in Kalamazoo County or adjacent areas
Consumers prioritizing NCUA-insured savings and community credit union values over national bank convenience
diagnosis: KALSEE Credit Union is best for residents or employees of 8 specific southwest Michigan counties seeking traditional credit union banking with NCUA protection and a 70-year institutional history. The primary caveat is strict geographic membership eligibility that excludes most consumers outside the defined service area, combined with unresolved customer service accessibility issues reported by existing members.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
